Canary Wharf Tower - Deal #CW-2024-007

Awaiting Approval

5-year lease term
Property
Canary Wharf Tower, Level 15-17

FinTech Solutions Ltd
Expansion • 450 employees

Document Version Comparison

Version 2.1

Current
Created: Oct 15, 2024 at 2:30 PM
Author: Max Chen
Status: Under Review
File: CW-2024-007-HoT-v2.1.pdf

Latest
Created: Oct 18, 2024 at 11:15 AM
Author: Legal Team

File: CW-2024-007-HoT-v2.2.pdf

Change Summary Overview

7
Items Added
12
Items Modified
3
Items Removed
18%
Overall Change

Critical Changes

  • • Rent escalation clause modified
  • • Break clause terms updated
  • • Service charge cap removed

Important Updates

  • • Fit-out contribution increased
  • • Parking allocation adjusted
  • • Maintenance responsibilities clarified

Minor Adjustments

  • • Contact details updated
  • • Formatting improvements
  • • Legal references corrected

Document Comparison

Added
Modified
Removed

Version 2.1 (Previous)

Current

1. Property Details

Address: Canary Wharf Tower, 42 Bank Street, London E14 5DA
Floor Area: 45,000 sq ft (Levels 15-17)
Parking: 25 spaces allocated

2. Financial Terms

Annual Rent: £2,250,000 per annum
Rent Reviews: Annual increases of 3% or RPI, whichever is higher
Service Charge: £450,000 per annum (estimated)
Service Charge Cap: Maximum 5% annual increase

3. Lease Terms

Lease Term: 5 years
Commencement: 1st April 2025
Break Clause: Tenant option at end of year 3 with 12 months notice

4. Tenant Improvements

Fit-out Contribution: £750,000 towards tenant improvements
Work Schedule: 12 weeks maximum for completion

Version 2.2 (Latest)

Latest

1. Property Details

Address: Canary Wharf Tower, 42 Bank Street, London E14 5DA
Floor Area: 45,000 sq ft (Levels 15-17)
Parking: 30 spaces allocated (5 additional executive spaces)
Storage: 2,000 sq ft basement storage included

2. Financial Terms

Annual Rent: £2,250,000 per annum
Rent Reviews: Annual increases of 2.5% fixed for first 3 years, then RPI
Service Charge: £450,000 per annum (estimated)
Utilities: Tenant responsible for direct utility contracts

3. Lease Terms

Lease Term: 5 years
Commencement: 1st April 2025
Break Clause: Tenant option at end of year 3 with 6 months notice
Renewal Option: 5-year renewal option at market rent

4. Tenant Improvements

Fit-out Contribution: £900,000 towards tenant improvements (increased)
Work Schedule: 12 weeks maximum for completion
Design Approval: Landlord approval required for major alterations

5. Additional Terms

Maintenance: Landlord responsible for structure and common areas
Insurance: Landlord to maintain building insurance, tenant for contents
Assignment: Permitted with landlord consent (not unreasonably withheld)

Detailed Change Analysis

Critical Changes Requiring Attention

3 items

Service Charge Cap Removed

Previous: Maximum 5% annual increase
New: No cap specified
High Financial Impact

Rent Escalation Modified

Previous: 3% or RPI, whichever is higher
New: 2.5% fixed for 3 years, then RPI
Medium Impact

Break Clause Notice Period Reduced

Previous: 12 months notice required
New: 6 months notice required
Favourable to Tenant

Beneficial Changes

4 items

Increased Fit-out Contribution

£750k → £900k (+£150k)

Additional Parking

25 → 30 spaces (+5 executive)

Storage Space Included

2,000 sq ft basement storage

Renewal Option Added

5-year renewal at market rate

Financial Impact Analysis

+£150k
Improved Fit-out
TBD
Service Charge Risk
-£45k
5-Year Rent Savings

5-Year Financial Projection

Year Previous Terms New Terms Difference Cumulative
Year 1 £2,317,500 £2,306,250 -£11,250 -£11,250
Year 2 £2,387,025 £2,363,906 -£23,119 -£34,369
Year 3 £2,458,636 £2,423,003 -£35,633 -£70,002
Year 4 £2,532,395 £2,520,563 -£11,832 -£81,834
Year 5 £2,608,367 £2,625,385 +£17,018 -£64,816
Net 5-Year Savings: £64,816

Excludes potential service charge increases due to removed cap

Request Redline

Request specific changes to problematic clauses

Lock for Signature

Finalize terms and prepare for e-signature

Comments & Collaboration

Sarah Johnson Legal Team • 2 hours ago

The removal of the service charge cap is concerning. This could expose the tenant to significant cost increases. @max-chen can we negotiate to reinstate a reasonable cap, perhaps 7% annually?

Max Chen Deal Architect • 1 hour ago

@sarah-johnson Good point. I'll add this to the redline requests. The increased fit-out contribution might give us leverage to negotiate the cap back in.

Executive Team Portfolio Navigator • 30 minutes ago

The overall terms look favourable. The additional storage and parking are valuable additions. Please proceed with addressing the service charge cap before final approval.
